mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances when unemployment rises > 0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power during recession onset.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative 5-day returns in > 50% of observations, or signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate when inflation exceeds threshold and rates are stable, or the inflation-rate interaction term shows no predictive IC.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with mean forward returns remaining flat or negative across all expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1% quarterly real GDP growth indicates severe demand contraction and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below threshold coincides with positive 20-day equity returns in > 65% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment signals a synchronized economic upturn, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
